boards.name: Cemetery Committee

meetings.summary:
# Cemetery Committee Meeting Summary

**Jul 25, 2025**

## Key Decisions
- Approved the minutes from the February 10, 2025 meeting
- Defeated motion to approve a special monument design for Brian Ulrick
- Defeated motion to approve a special monument design for Jerry Reichstein
- Adjourned the meeting at 10:23 a.m.

## Main Topics
- The Committee reviewed and approved previous meeting minutes
- Two requests for custom monument designs were considered but not approved by the Committee

## Notable Items
- Both special monument design requests were voted against and did not proceed
- The next regular Cemetery Committee meeting will be held no later than February 28, 2026

---
*Next regular meeting: no later than February 28, 2026*
meetings.title: Cemetery Committee

meetings.summary:
# Cemetery Committee Meeting Summary

**February 10, 2025**

## Key Decisions
- Dave Yemm appointed as Chairperson for 2025
- Approved purchase and installation of 75 feet of ornamental fence (5 feet high) along the western edge of the old section of Nipissing Union Cemetery from NEFAF Inc.
- Authorized continued investigation into expanding St. John's Alsace Cemetery, including a complete property survey and application to the Bereavement Authority of Ontario for Care and Maintenance Funds
- Approved removal of a spruce tree at St. John's Alsace Cemetery due to safety concerns and authorized hiring an arborist for the work
- Supported the 2025 draft Cemetery operational budget for inclusion in Township budget discussions and Council approval

## Main Topics
- **Nipissing Union Cemetery:** The Committee reviewed the 2025 work plan and approved fencing improvements along the western edge of the older burial section.
- **St. John's Alsace Cemetery:** Two matters were addressed—a safety issue involving a large spruce tree near the entrance that requires professional removal, and a longer-term project to explore expanding the cemetery grounds through surveying and seeking provincial funding approval.
- **Budget:** The Committee reviewed and endorsed the draft 2025 Cemetery operational budget for Council consideration.

## Notable Items
- Ornamental fence installation will improve the appearance and definition of Nipissing Union Cemetery's old section
- St. John's Alsace Cemetery expansion project requires Bereavement Authority of Ontario approval before proceeding
- Arborist services will be contracted to safely remove the hazardous spruce tree at St. John's Alsace Cemetery
- Next regular meeting scheduled no later than February 28, 2026

---
*Next regular meeting: no later than February 28, 2026*

boards.description: Administration of local cemeteries in Nipissing Township.

boards.name: Museum Board

meetings.summary:
# Museum Board Meeting Summary

**April 1, 2026**

## Key Decisions
- Approved minutes from the March 4, 2026 meeting
- Approved opening all workshop time slots to residents of all ages to improve access
- Approved proposed workshop schedule that allows staff setup, cleanup time, and visitor museum tours
- Decided to have students lead workshops with support from Museum Manager and Board members
- Approved plan to take basket-making workshop to the library in fall 2026, following summer feedback
- Scheduled rope crafts training session for Board volunteers before the next meeting

## Main Topics
- **Building repairs and inspection**: Foundation repairs are complete; staff will schedule an inspection before the 2026 season opening
- **Workshop planning**: The Board discussed timing, age accessibility, and logistics for upcoming butter-making, ice-cream-making, and rope-crafts workshops. Posters for butter and ice-cream workshops will be distributed by Board members
- **Heritage Day**: The Board reviewed 2025 attendance numbers and discussed fundraising ideas for the August 2026 event. They requested that Fire Halls display promotional signs closer to the event date
- **Exhibits and programs**: Work continues on "The Waterway Journey to Nipissing" exhibit and the Remembrance Interactive program. The Board received positive feedback on the Streams and Ladders game distributed during the 2025 Fire Department Christmas Run

## Notable Items
- Museum season opening is approaching; foundation inspection is pending
- Rope crafts training session will occur before the next Board meeting
- Workshop posters will be available at the May meeting for community distribution
- Heritage Day fundraising and event details require further discussion at the next meeting
- Artifact cataloguing and data entry will resume during the museum season

---
*Next regular meeting: Wednesday, May 6, 2026 at 6:00 p.m.*

# Museum Board Meeting Summary

**Mar 4, 2026**

## Key Decisions
- Jane Aultman appointed as Chairperson for 2026
- November 5, 2025 meeting minutes approved
- Board adjourned at 6:18 p.m.

## Main Topics
- **Building inspection**: An engineer will inspect the Museum before it opens for the season.
- **Event planning and volunteers**: Board members are being asked to volunteer for scheduled events. Summer students will help support events. The band "Country Re-Runs and Friends" was suggested as live entertainment for Heritage Day.
- **Exhibit development**: Staff have been developing "The Waterway Journey to Nipissing" as the theme for Heritage Day and a Remembrance Interactive exhibit program. The Board discussed applying for grants to create a traveling Remembrance Day exhibit that could visit the Powassan Library after being installed at the Museum.
- **Cataloguing**: Artifact cataloguing and data entry will resume during the Museum season.
- **Community feedback**: The Board is gathering feedback on a museum-designed game used during the 2025 Christmas event through Facebook and community discussion to guide future projects.

## Notable Items
- Engineer inspection of the Museum is scheduled before opening
- Heritage Day will feature "The Waterway Journey to Nipissing" theme
- Remembrance Interactive exhibit will be installed at the Museum this year to test it before potential grant-funded traveling exhibit
- Summer students will support seasonal events

---
*Next regular meeting: Wednesday, April 1, 2026 at 6:00 p.m.*
meetings.title: Museum Board

meetings.summary:
# Museum Board Meeting Summary

**November 5, 2025**

## Key Decisions
- Approved minutes from the October 1, 2025 meeting
- Approved the 2026 Museum Budget request ($21,100 in expenses, $6,300 in projected revenue) for submission to Council

## Main Topics
- **Building repairs completed**: Foundation work finished over two weeks, including block work, insulation, drainage, and backfill. A heater will be installed to maintain minimum temperature per engineer recommendations.
- **Walkway maintenance**: Weeds pulled and walkways edged during museum closing procedures.
- **Artifact donations**: Accepted artifacts have been received and will be catalogued in the upcoming season. The museum is still awaiting pictures of postcards from North Bay Museum for potential donation.
- **Exhibit planning**: Work underway on "Nipissing along the Waterway" theme for Heritage Day, expanding on the previous roads exhibit. Board discussed adding maps to museum buildings for visitor reference and planning a Remembrance Day travelling exhibit for next year.
- **2025 budget review**: Revenue tracking at $4,883.24 against $9,150 budgeted; expenses at $50,388.95 against $66,385 budgeted.

## Notable Items
- Foundation repairs and heater installation align with the museum's strategic plan goals
- 2026 budget request of $21,100 in expenses (down from 2025 budgeted amount of $66,385) reflects completion of major structural work
- Heater installation scheduled for the week following the meeting

---
*Next regular meeting: Wednesday, March 4, 2026 at 6:00 p.m.*

# Museum Board Meeting Summary

**October 1, 2025**

## Key Decisions
- Approved September 3, 2025 meeting minutes
- Set 2026 museum season to open July 1 and close September 6
- Postponed the 2026 Remembrance Day event to allow more planning time; will set up a temporary exhibit in the office building instead
- Approved 2026 workshop schedule: Butter Making (July 2 & 4), Ice Cream Making (July 18), and Ropework Crafts (August 13 & 15)
- Selected "The Journey to Nipissing via the Waterways & Beginning of the Settlement" as the Heritage Day 2026 theme, scheduled for Sunday August 9
- Accepted two new artifacts for the collection: a Phonograph Record Player with Records and a Child's Sock Stretcher

## Main Topics
- **Building repairs**: Council approved foundation repairs to the museum, to be completed by a local contractor and township staff in fall 2025. This will enable the board to apply for grant funding for other museum improvements.
- **2026 season planning**: The board discussed opening in June to accommodate school groups and special interest groups, with board members assisting the manager for pre-season bookings. The motion to open July 1 was carried.
- **Community engagement**: The Fire Department produced 80 fabric copies of a "Streams and Ladders" game designed by the museum for the Christmas Run; remaining copies will be sold in 2026.
- **Workshops**: Proposed workshops will cost $15 or less to ensure accessibility, based on material cost recovery. Workshops can be offered offsite at locations like the library or Commanda Museum.
- **Artifact cataloguing**: All 2025 donations have been accessioned and entered into the PastPerfect catalogue system; some backlogged items are also being processed.

## Notable Items
- 2026 museum season runs July 1 to September 6
- Workshop fees capped at $15 to keep programs accessible
- Heritage Day 2026 is August 9, focusing on early settlement and waterway history
- Foundation repairs will be completed fall 2025, enabling future grant applications
- 2025 revenue totaled $4,727.24

---
*Next regular meeting: Wednesday, November 5, 2025 at 6:00 p.m.*

# Museum Board Meeting Summary

**September 3, 2025**

## Key Decisions

- Approved August 6, 2025 meeting minutes
- Approved selling remaining candy at reduced price at Recreation Committee Trunk Sale on September 13, with leftover candy to be donated to Township Fire Department for Halloween event
- Approved converting the November 2, 2025 Remembrance Day event from a single-location event to a travelling interactive exhibit
- Accepted donation of a psychrometer from Mike Brewers to the Museum collections
- Deferred decisions on three artifact donations (horse-drawn grader, Royal Black Institution sash, and photo postcards) to future meetings

## Main Topics

- **2025 Season Performance**: The Museum generated $4,707.49 in revenue through August, with 369 total visitors. Special events brought in 206 visitors while regular museum visits accounted for 182 visitors. Heritage Day was the most successful event, generating $737.96 in net revenue with community support for donation-based BBQ meals.

- **Building Repairs**: Staff will continue investigating grant opportunities and other funding options to address needed foundation repairs at the Museum building.

- **Remembrance Day Event Changes**: The Board decided to shift the November 2 event to a travelling interactive exhibit format due to scheduling and planning constraints. Council has already approved the grant application for this approach. The Board will discuss training for members who may travel with the exhibit at the next meeting.

- **2026 Planning**: The Board began preliminary discussions about workshops, events, and staffing for the 2026 season, including possible variations to Museum opening dates to accommodate special interest groups and cataloguing work.

- **Merchandise Opportunities**: The Fire Department is testing a Snakes and Ladders game for their Christmas Run; if suitable, the Museum Board may add it to 2026 merchandise offerings.

## Notable Items

- Trunk Sale event scheduled for September 13, 2025
- Remembrance Day travelling exhibit to launch November 2, 2025
- Maple Syrup exhibit now permanently installed in the Hardware Store
- 2025 revenue to date: $4,707.49
- 2025 visitor count to date: 369 (206 at special events, 182 regular visitors)
- Heritage Day BBQ averaged $8.00 per meal in donations

---

*Next regular meeting: Wednesday, October 1, 2025 at 6:00 p.m.*

# Museum Board Meeting Summary

**August 6, 2025**

## Key Decisions
- Approved July 2, 2025 meeting minutes
- Accepted four artifact donations: duck decoys and declined outboard motor from Phil Rimmer; three trivets from Bernadete Kerr; mechanical screwdriver from Mike Brewers; wringer washer from Dorothy Pryce
- Approved Heritage Day food pricing: hamburgers, sausages, and hot dogs offered by donation with included drinks
- Decided to pause artifact cataloguing into PastPerfect system during exhibit planning

## Main Topics
- **July Financial Performance**: Museum generated $1,183.55 in revenue from events (Tea & Talk, Butter Making, gift shop sales, and donations) with 168 total visitors. Net revenue after event costs was $131.84.
- **Building Maintenance**: Ontario Trillium Foundation grant for museum office foundation repairs was declined; staff will pursue alternative funding. Hardware store lighting has been upgraded and is now more effective.
- **Community Events**: Tea & Talk event received positive feedback; Stitch & Sip weekly drop-in craft sessions are running Wednesday afternoons; Butter Making event attracted 13 participants ages 5–60; Commanda SummerFest outreach reached 34 visitors; Basket Making workshop has 24 registered participants.
- **Exhibits**: Maple Syrup exhibit installed in Hardware Store; "Building the Homestead" exhibit being set up as permanent display for Heritage Day theme; Remembrance Day interactive exhibit in development using choose-your-own-adventure format with local WW I and WW II stories.
- **Strategic Plan**: New merchandise received but sales expected lower due to reduced accessibility in church storage space. Activity workshops successfully raising community awareness and visitor engagement.

## Notable Items
- Heritage Day event scheduled with Rye Runners providing 3 hours of live music and Fire Department BBQ
- Basket Making survey will be distributed to gather feedback for future workshop planning
- Board discussed need for better artifact storage space and deaccession practices
- Commanda SummerFest identified as potential annual outreach opportunity requiring one staff member and one board member

---
*Next regular meeting: Wednesday, September 3, 2025 at 6:00 p.m.*

# Museum Board Meeting Summary

**July 2, 2025**

## Key Decisions
- Approved June 4, 2025 meeting minutes
- Approved two summer activity days: butter making on July 19th ($5 per participant) and basket making on August 7th ($10 per participant)
- Authorized use of Museum Program budget funds to purchase supplies for the activity days
- Adjourned meeting at 6:29 p.m.

## Main Topics
- **Building repairs**: The Office/Candy Store building needs block bracing work before the public can use it. Staff and one person at a time may access the building for washroom use only until repairs are complete. A contractor has been contacted. Temporary candy and merchandise sales have been moved to the Church building.
- **Summer events**: The Museum will participate in SummerFest on July 26th (noon to 8 p.m.) hosted by Commanda Museum, with a tent provided by the Fire Department. The Library exhibit was successful; a games night had lower attendance possibly due to weather and scheduling.
- **Upcoming programs**: Tea & Talk event has secured presenters and sourced food. Stitch and Sip in the Gazebo will run Wednesday afternoons (1–3 p.m.) for free drop-in attendance.
- **Exhibits and artifacts**: Interactive displays about maple syrup production are being set up. Two new items offered to the museum (1960 outboard motor and paper mâché duck decoy) will be considered at the next meeting. Artifact cataloguing is paused during exhibit planning.

## Notable Items
- Office/Candy Store building repairs must be completed before public access is permitted
- SummerFest participation on July 26th; Museum Manager to confirm available space
- Activity day costs: $5 for butter making (July 19th), $10 for basket making (August 7th)
- Stitch and Sip program runs free every Wednesday afternoon through the season

---
*Next regular meeting: Wednesday, August 6, 2025 at 6:00 p.m.*

# Museum Board Meeting Summary

**June 4, 2025**

## Key Decisions
- Approved May 7, 2025 meeting minutes
- Closed the museum on Saturday, July 26, 2025 to allow staff to participate in SummerFest at Commanda Museum
- Invited Commanda Museum to participate in Heritage Day Celebration on August 17, 2025
- Donated museum merchandise to Commanda Museum for SummerFest silent auction
- Approved 2025 Heritage Day flyer
- Approved hiring a live band for Heritage Day event at a maximum cost of $500
- Donated prizes to Powassan and District Library for Museum Board Game Night on June 19, 2025

## Main Topics
- **Building readiness**: The Office/Candy Store building received engineer approval to open for the season, with Public Works completing air conditioning and water setup.
- **Summer events**: Museum Games Night scheduled for June 19 (5:00–7:00 p.m.) at Powassan Library, featuring student-created games and museum collection items. SummerFest participation planned for July 26 at Commanda Museum (noon–8 p.m.).
- **Heritage Day planning**: August 17 event will feature a live band, interactive maple syrup production exhibits, and a Tea and Talk event with preparation work on July 12.
- **Artifact management**: Cataloguing work paused during exhibit planning. Artifacts stored at Community Centre will be moved back to the museum as a priority, with board member assistance requested.

## Notable Items
- Museum Games Night: June 19, 2025, 5:00–7:00 p.m. at Powassan Library
- SummerFest: July 26, 2025, noon–8 p.m. (museum closed this day)
- Heritage Day: August 17, 2025 (live band budget: $500 maximum)
- Tea and Talk prep session: Saturday, July 12, 2025 (time to be confirmed)

---
*Next regular meeting: Wednesday, July 2, 2025 at 6:00 p.m.*

# Museum Board Meeting Summary

**May 7, 2025**

## Key Decisions
- Approved minutes from the March 5, 2025 meeting
- Agreed to explore closing the museum on July 26 to allow staff and students to participate in the SummerFest event at Commanda Museum (motion to be formalized at next meeting)
- Agreed to explore inviting Commanda Museum to participate in Heritage Day on August 17, 2025 (motion to be formalized at next meeting)
- Paused artifact cataloguing into PastPerfect system during exhibit planning phase

## Main Topics
- **Building maintenance**: The parking lot was improved with gravel added by Public Works. Councillor Kirkey offered to treat weeds in the walkway and add sand to fill cracks.
- **Summer events**: The museum was invited to participate in SummerFest on July 26 at no cost. The board discussed whether to close the museum that day so all staff and students can attend and showcase the museum.
- **Library exhibit**: A new exhibit featuring student-created games (snakes and ladders, habitats card game) and Lake Nipissing themes will be displayed at Powassan and District Library in May and June. Feedback forms and promotional materials will be available.
- **Strategic Plan**: The board received an update on ongoing and completed initiatives. Future progress reports will be provided regularly, and the plan will be reformatted with photos for presentation to Council.
- **National Volunteer Week**: The board recognized National Volunteer Week (April 27 – May 3, 2025) with the theme "Volunteers Make Waves."

## Notable Items
- Tea and Talk event has been advertised on Facebook and North Bay Now
- Museum is requesting to borrow a pop-up tent from the Fire Department for the SummerFest event
- Feedback forms will be collected from library visitors about the student-created games
- Strategic Plan progress reports will be provided at future meetings

---
*Next regular meeting: Wednesday, June 4, 2025 at 6:00 p.m.*

boards.description: Preservation and display of the history and heritage of Nipissing Township.

boards.name: Recreation Committee

meetings.summary:
# Recreation Committee Meeting Summary

**March 2, 2026**

## Key Decisions
- Approved February 2, 2026 meeting minutes
- Designated $195.00 in Chair Yoga donations to fund 2 additional yoga classes and support the Powassan & Callander Food Banks
- Extended the Winter Cards Program through the end of March 2026
- Approved adjustments to next year's Spaghetti Dinner: moving time to 4:00–7:00 p.m., increasing meatballs from 3 to 4 cases, and increasing garlic bread quantity

## Main Topics
- **Fundraising**: Bottle drive collected $364.80 total ($226.80 from Bearcreek Landfill and $138.00 in cash donations). Wolfe Lake Landfill collection is pending rescheduling.
- **Upcoming Events**: Easter Event (April 4th), Trivia Night (March 21st), Soccer Season (May 7–June 25th), and Mother's Day Craft (date to be determined, possibly cancelled).
- **Program Feedback**: Corn Hole and Cards programs are well-attended and popular. Corn Hole timing will shift to 7:00–9:00 p.m. with potential for outdoor expansion in warmer months.
- **Recent Event Reviews**: Family Skate drew 180 people and came in under budget ($1,623.22 of $1,700.00). Spaghetti Dinner served 97 people and raised $1,403.25.

## Notable Items
- Easter Event moved indoors to Community Centre to accommodate weather
- Soccer registration closes April 15, 2026; season runs May 7–June 25 for ages 4–12
- Chair Yoga final session: April 20, 2026
- Trivia Night: March 21, 2026 at 7:00 p.m. ($20 per team)
- Family Skate leftover marshmallows will be used at Easter Event

---
*Next regular meeting: May 4, 2026*

# Recreation Committee Meeting Summary

**Jan 19, 2026**

## Key Decisions
- Calista Bateman appointed as Chairperson for 2026
- November 17, 2025 meeting minutes approved
- Motion passed to distribute $425.45 in Chair Yoga donations: $150 to Powassan Food Bank, $150 to Callander Food Bank, and $125.45 to Recreation Account
- 2026 meeting dates deferred for staff review due to scheduling conflicts and community programming needs
- New winter yoga program discussion postponed to next meeting pending additional information

## Main Topics
- **Meeting scheduling**: Committee discussed potential conflicts with November 30 and the impact of evening meetings on community programs like yoga. Staff will review calendar options and return with alternatives.
- **Chair Yoga program**: Successfully resumed with 12 sessions scheduled. First 10 sessions funded by the committee; remaining two funded by donations. Average attendance is 23–24 participants. Defibrillator orientation was provided.
- **Bottle drive fundraiser**: Collected $363.40 from both landfill locations. Committee thanked volunteers including Councillors Stephen Kirkey and James Scott.
- **Spaghetti Dinner**: Upcoming event with ticket prices at $15 (adults) and $8 (children). Goal is to break even while building community engagement. Menu includes spaghetti, meatballs, garlic bread, and desserts. Volunteers confirmed and promotion planned via flyers, signage, and social media.
- **Family Skate event**: Confirmed fireworks, hot chocolate and marshmallow station, and participation by Powassan Voodoos. DJ confirmation pending. Outdoor ice conditions reported as excellent.

## Notable Items
- Chair Yoga donations totaling $425.45 distributed to local food banks and recreation account
- Bottle drive raised $363.40 from community volunteers
- Chair Yoga averaging 23–24 participants per session
- Spaghetti Dinner ticket prices: $15 adults, $8 children
- New winter yoga program details to be reviewed at next meeting

---
*Next regular meeting: March 2, 2026*
meetings.title: Recreation Committee

meetings.summary:
# Recreation Committee Meeting Summary

**November 17, 2025**

## Key Decisions
- Approved October 6, 2025 meeting minutes
- Approved $500 donation from the Recreation Event Programming Budget to the Township of Nipissing Fire Department for the December 13, 2025 Christmas Run
- Agreed to bring a motion forward at the December meeting to submit the 2026 proposed budget ($15,500 for events and programming plus $5,000 for Canada Day) to Council for inclusion in the municipal budget
- Discussed consolidating teen dances from two separate age-group events into one combined event for ages 11–15

## Main Topics
- **Bottle Drive Results**: November collection totaled $812.50 ($798.10 from landfills, $14.40 in donations)
- **Teen Dance Event**: Successfully held with 28 attendees; DJ took music requests and costume prizes were awarded; canteen raised $60
- **Halloween Event**: Two stations served 105 total attendees (56 at Station 1, 49 at Station 2)
- **Upcoming Baking/Craft Event**: Holiday centerpiece workshop scheduled for December 6 at $15 per person; event filled quickly with a waitlist in place
- **2026 Recreation Budget**: Committee reviewed proposed spending across 14 events and programs, with several events designated as cost-neutral (participant fees cover expenses)

## Notable Items
- December 6, 2025: Holiday centerpiece baking/craft event (fully registered with waitlist)
- December 13, 2025: Christmas Run (Fire Department event receiving $500 committee donation)
- 2026 proposed budget totals $20,500 ($15,500 for events/programming plus $5,000 for Canada Day)
- Budget approval motion scheduled for December 15 meeting before forwarding to Council

---
*Next regular meeting: December 15, 2025*

# Recreation Committee Meeting Summary

**October 6, 2025**

## Key Decisions
- Approved purchase of $40 in prize gift cards (McDonald's and Dairy Queen) for the Halloween Teen Dance
- Approved $200 spending for Halloween candy donations to Fire Stations 1 and 2 for their October 31 event
- Approved purchase of a commercial seated recumbent stepper machine for the Fitness Centre, funded by private donation
- Approved September 15, 2025 meeting minutes

## Main Topics
- **Bottle Drive Results**: Collected $186.10 at Wolfe Lake Landfill and $212.10 at Bear Creek Landfill. Committee is working to recruit more volunteers for future collections.
- **Safe Food Handling Course**: North Bay Health Unit course currently has only 2 registrations but needs 15 minimum to run. The Community Centre is waiving rental fees as part of the Township's Community Safety and Well-Being plan.
- **Teen Dance Event**: Organized into two age groups (11-13 and 14-16) with separate time slots. Committee will promote the event through outdoor signage, website, social media, and school flyers.
- **Fitness Centre Equipment**: Received a resident donation to purchase new exercise equipment, which will be maintained under the annual maintenance program.

## Notable Items
- Halloween Teen Dance and Fire Station candy events scheduled for Friday, October 31, 2025
- Committee members asked to bring 2026 programming ideas and budget proposals to the next meeting for budget planning
- Safe Food Handling Course needs 13 more registrations to proceed

---
*Next regular meeting: November 17, 2025*

# Recreation Committee Meeting Summary

**September 15, 2025**

## Key Decisions
- Approved June 9, 2025 meeting minutes
- Approved use of the Community Centre Hall on the third Wednesday of each month (1:00–4:00 p.m.) for Community Clinics hosted by the District of Parry Sound Social Services Administration Board
- Appointed a Chair for the meeting

## Main Topics
- **Bottle Drive**: Collected $3,069.80 from June through September at Wolfe Lake and Bear Creek Landfill. The committee is working to recruit more volunteers for future collections.
- **Soccer Season**: Extended 2 weeks into July due to weather challenges. The committee plans to start the season earlier in May next year and noted difficulty finding coaches and referees.
- **Canada Day Event**: Spent $3,203.86 of a $5,000 budget. Event included fireworks, DJ, entertainment (balloon art, face painting, and a magician), and cupcakes. Feedback was positive.
- **Summer Programs**: Movie Night drew 50+ people; Trivia Night raised $176.00; Drop In Sports Night had low attendance (3 kids) but allowed time to clean and reorganize the Rink Shack. Chair Yoga will resume September 29.
- **Trunk Sale**: 15 vendors registered with 4 no-shows. The event went well with good foot traffic and live music from Rye Runners.
- **Outdoor Rink Upgrade**: New wood and puck board have been purchased. Public Works is leveling the pad and repositioning light poles.

## Notable Items
- Bottle drive collection occurs the Sunday before each monthly meeting
- Tom Morrow Powassan Home Hardware donated $121.50 from movie night and trunk sale proceeds
- Chair Yoga fall session resumes September 29, 2025
- Picnic table options and pricing for Links Beach will be presented at a future meeting
- Community Clinics will use the Community Centre Hall starting the third Wednesday of each month at 1:00 p.m.

---
*Next regular meeting: October 6, 2025*

# Recreation Committee Meeting Summary

**June 9, 2025**

## Key Decisions
- Approved the Rye Runners live band for the Music Market on July 24, 2025, at a cost of $375.00
- Approved "Lilo & Stitch" as the movie for Movie Night on September 6, 2025
- Approved adding 2 more Chair Yoga classes based on strong spring session donations of $329.75
- May 12, 2025 meeting minutes approved as circulated

## Main Topics
- **Bottle Drive**: Collected $511.90 total ($264.70 from Wolfe Lake, $247.20 from Bear Creek). Committee is working to recruit more volunteers for future collections.
- **Soccer**: Two Thursday sessions were rained out. Committee received positive feedback about extending the season. Facing a shortage of coaches and referees; will ask parents if they can help when needed. End of Season BBQ scheduled for June 26, 2025.
- **Canada Day Event**: Fireworks ordered, DJ George Winters confirmed, cupcakes ordered, and entertainment booked including Tickled Teals (balloon art and face painting) and Magic of Christophe.
- **Trivia Night**: Wrap-up showed $176.00 total revenue ($153.50 from registration and canteen, $22.50 in donations). George Winters praised as valuable asset to the event.
- **Chair Yoga**: Fall session to begin either the last Monday of September or first Monday of October. Committee recommends increased promotion through Facebook posts and printed flyers.

## Notable Items
- Music Market with live band: July 24, 2025
- Movie Night: September 6, 2025
- Canada Day event: Details finalized with entertainment and food
- Chair Yoga expansion: 2 additional classes approved; fall session start date to be confirmed
- Trivia Night generated $176.00 revenue

---
*Next regular meeting: July 14, 2025*

# Recreation Committee Meeting Summary

**May 12, 2025**

## Key Decisions
- Approved minutes from the March 3, 2025 meeting
- Confirmed soccer program is ready to launch with 90 children registered
- Set End of Season Soccer BBQ for June 26, 2025
- Scheduled Music Market event for July 24, 2025 (6–9 p.m. at Heritage Park)
- Scheduled Trunk Sale for September 13, 2025 (9 a.m.–1 p.m.)

## Main Topics
- **Fundraising results**: Bottle drive collected $707.10 total; Spaghetti Dinner made a small profit of $80.30 after expenses
- **Recent events**: Easter event on April 19 had 24 children attend (28 registered) with egg hunt and take-home flower pots; Scone Baking Event on May 10 had 8 participants
- **Soccer program**: 90 kids registered, coaches meeting held May 8, equipment and recreation shed prepared
- **Upcoming summer events**: Music Market in July will feature live music (potentially the Manella Sisters); vendors welcome with 10×10 booth spaces; Trunk Sale in September also accepting vendors
- **Kitchen supplies**: Committee identified need for additional kitchen items for future events

## Notable Items
- Soccer season ends with BBQ on June 26, 2025
- Music Market save-the-date flyer in development; live music quotes being obtained
- Trunk Sale and Music Market both require vendor registration through the township office
- Community yard sale in Commanda area on May 25, 2025 (look for red balloons to identify sales)
- Kitchen equipment gaps to be addressed before next events

---
*Next regular meeting: June 9, 2025*

# Recreation Committee Meeting Summary

**March 3, 2025**

## Key Decisions
- Shelly Foote appointed as Chairperson for the meeting
- Winter Drop-In Cards program cancelled effective March 3, 2025 due to lack of participation (zero attendance after four sessions)
- Approved up to $200 in funding to purchase prizes for Trivia Night
- Approved purchase of three pieces of fitness centre equipment through reserves: a treadmill, elliptical, and bike

## Main Topics
- **Fundraising**: Bottle drive collected $273.90 ($91.90 from Wolfe Lake, $182.00 from Bear Creek)
- **Recent events**: Family Day drew 70–80 people with skating and fireworks; Spaghetti Dinner sold 69 meals with 12 volunteers
- **Upcoming spring and summer events**: Trivia Night (registration opens March 4), Family Easter Fun Day (April 19), Baking Event (May 10), Teen Dance (June 13), Canada Day celebration (June 28), and Music Market
- **Fitness centre**: Discussion about equipment replacement strategy and future security system needs (not budgeted this year)
- **Community partner**: Commanda Community Spring Dance scheduled for April 26 at 7:00 p.m.

## Notable Items
- Trivia Night registration opens March 4, 2025
- Family Easter Fun Day registration: March 14 to April 4, 2025
- Baking Event registration: April 1 to April 30, 2025 (16 spots available at $10 per registration)
- Teen Dance will split into two age groups: ages 11–13 (5:30–7:15 p.m.) and ages 14–16 (7:30–10:00 p.m.)
- Canada Day event will feature DJ, vendors (balloon artist, face painter, magician, reptile camp), fireworks, and cupcakes

---
*Next regular meeting: April 7, 2025*

# Recreation Committee Meeting Summary

**February 3, 2025**

## Key Decisions
- Approved January 6, 2025 meeting minutes
- Approved 10-week Chair Yoga Program to continue
- Rescheduled Trivia Night from March 29, 2025 to April 5, 2025
- Meeting adjourned at 6:49 p.m.

## Main Topics
- **Bottle Drive fundraiser** collected $351.90 total ($60.40 from Wolfe Lake, $291.50 from Bear Creek)
- **Fall 2024 Chair Yoga** ran September 30 to December 16 with average attendance of 16 people and raised $171.35 plus 87 lbs of food for local food banks
- **Winter Cards drop-in program** runs January 9 to March 20 every second Thursday from 2:00–4:00 p.m. at the Community Centre; no participants yet but committee is promoting through flyers and reminders
- **Family Day event** scheduled for February 16, 6:00–8:00 p.m. with DJ, fireworks at 7:00 p.m., hot chocolate, marshmallows, fire pits, and activities on the rink
- **Spaghetti Dinner** planning underway with estimated total cost of $642.61; committee will seek local business donations for door prizes and needs volunteers at 2:30 p.m.
- **Commanda Community Centre** offering 50/50 raffle, free "Stay on your Feet" sessions Fridays at 10:00 a.m., and Thursday yoga classes at $10.00

## Notable Items
- Winter Cards program has zero participants so far; committee expanding promotion beyond Facebook to include in-store flyers
- Family Day rink is in good condition and ready for the event
- Spaghetti Dinner ticket price remains the same as last year
- Commanda Community Centre 50/50 raffle (License #RAF1439034) supports museum maintenance

---
*Next regular meeting: March 3, 2025*
